focus: Orienting the review within the Fourth Industrial Revolution context and establishing the scope of AI's transformative impact on labor markets.
title: Introduction and Scope

focus: Theoretical models of task automation, displacement, and reinstatement, emphasizing Acemoglu and Restrepo's contributions.
title: Task-Based Frameworks and Displacement-Reinstatement

focus: Measures of occupational exposure to AI, from early computerization probabilities to recent large language model vulnerability assessments.
title: Occupational Exposure and Susceptibility to AI

focus: Studies examining actual employment effects, hiring changes, and labor demand shifts due to AI and generative AI, spanning cross-country and online labor market data.
title: Empirical Evidence on Employment and Labor Demand

focus: Experimental and quasi-experimental evidence on individual and firm-level productivity gains from generative AI in writing, coding, and customer service tasks.
title: Productivity Effects of Generative AI

focus: How AI adoption reshapes skill premiums, wage inequality, and job polarization, influencing demand for high-skill versus routine and low-skill workers.
title: Wage Inequality, Skill Demand, and Labor Polarization

focus: Policy responses including labor regulations, social safety nets, reskilling initiatives, and international strategies to ensure inclusive gains from AI.
title: Policy Implications and Frameworks for Managing Disruption

focus: A cross-cutting analysis of convergent and conflicting findings across studies, highlighting the early and fragmented state of evidence.
title: Cross-Study Synthesis and Contradictions

focus: Summary of collectively supported insights and identification of critical unresolved questions, such as long-term general equilibrium effects and impacts on developing economies.
title: Conclusions and Research Gaps
